cont_clean: Scammers are using voice-cloning technology to swindle people out of their hard-earned money. The US Federal Trade Commission has issued a warning on this emerging voice cloning scam, reminding people that the voice of a friend or relative asking for money may actually be an AI simulacrum wielded by a scammer. Geriatrics in Canada are a particularly vulnerable demographic, with at least eight senior citizens targeted by these voice cloning scams, making away with a total $200,000 CAD in just a three-day window. Protect yourself by reaching out to your supposed relative by using a phone number you know belongs to them and watch out for hard-to-trace means of sending money. #datafunction #VoiceCloningScam #FTC #ProtectYourself #Scammers #AI #EmergingThreat

cont_clean: Echoes of Deception The rain drummed against Harriette's apartment windows as she stared at her phone, her hands trembling. What she heard on her voicemail was unmistakably her voice, yet she'd never made that call begging her grandmother for emergency money. Three other friends had just texted, asking about similar suspicious calls they'd received. Her throat tightened as she realized what had happened. Those podcast interviews she'd done for her small business – someone had used them to clone her voice. The AI-generated calls had already convinced her cousin to send $2,000 to a scammer! Harriette's fingers flew across her keyboard as she documented everything. She recorded new voicemails for her contacts, explaining the situation: "If you get a call from me asking for money, it's not real. It's an AI clone." Her voice cracked, but she kept recording. She contacted the police, her bank, and the FBI's Internet Crime Complaint Center. Then she did something unexpected – she went public. On her business's social media, she shared her story, explaining how voice cloning worked and warning others. Her post went viral within her community. Local news picked up the story. Instead of hiding in shame, Harriette turned her ordeal into a crusade. She organized workshops with senior centers about AI scam awareness. Her grandmother proudly attended each one. Two months later, on another rainy Sunday, Harriette smiled as she received a different kind of call – a tech journalist wanting to interview her about her advocacy work. This time, when her voice would be recorded, it would be on her own terms, helping others protect themselves from the very technology that had once been used against her. The rain still fell outside, but it no longer felt threatening. She had transformed her fear into power, her silence into a voice of warning and hope for others. #voicecloningscam #protectyourvoice #awareness #staysafe

Scammers are now using Artificial Intelligence (AI) to replicate anyone's voice using just a few seconds of audio stolen from social media. According to the FBI, Americans lost a jaw-dropping $893 million to AI-related scams last year alone, including these terrifying voice cloning and phishing attacks. Tech gets so realistic that even experts struggle to spot the difference between a real human and an AI clone. How to Protect Yourself & Your Family: 🔐 Create a Family 'Code Word': Set up a secret word or phrase with your loved ones that is never shared online. If you get a suspicious distress call, ask for the code word! 🛑 Spot the Red Flags: Be highly skeptical if the caller creates extreme urgency, demands large sums of money, or begs you not to tell anyone else. 📞 Hang Up and Cross-Check: Never transfer money immediately. Hang up and text or call your loved one through a different device or contact someone who is with them to confirm their safety. A familiar voice is not always proof. Pause. Verify. cont_clean: A phone call beat the perimeter. The Charter/Spectrum incident is a warning for CIOs and CISOs: attackers do not always need a software flaw when they can compromise trust. PKWARE reports the breach path involved vishing against an employee, leading to Microsoft Entra account access and CRM exposure. SecurityWeek reported ShinyHunters' larger record claims, while Have I Been Pwned identified about 4.9M unique emails. Charter said no sensitive PI or CPNI was released. The executive takeaway: identity is now the perimeter. That means cyber strategy has to move beyond devices and tools into: • Identity governance • Phishing-resistant MFA • Conditional access • Privileged access controls • CRM export monitoring • Security awareness under pressure • Vendor and SaaS access review Before adding another security platform, ask the harder question: Which identities can reach sensitive data, and why? cont_clean: 🚨 ¡ALERTA DE SEGURIDAD! Desconfía si te piden marcar el *21 Los ciberdelincuentes están usando una nueva modalidad de estafa telefónica (Vishing). Se hacen pasar por ejecutivos bancarios o soporte técnico y, bajo una falsa urgencia (como un supuesto fraude en tu cuenta), te solicitan marcar el código *21 seguido de un número de teléfono. ¿Qué pasa si lo haces? Al marcar ese código, activas el desvío de llamadas hacia el teléfono del atacante. Con esto, ellos pueden: • Interceptar tus llamadas telefónicas. • Recibir tus códigos de verificación por voz (2FA). • Validar transferencias bancarias y tomar el control de tus cuentas. 🛡️ ¿Cómo protegerte? 1. Ninguna institución legítima te pedirá marcar códigos en tu celular para solucionar un problema. 2. Si te genera dudas, cuelga de inmediato y llama tú mismo al número oficial de tu banco. 3. Si crees que caíste en el engaño, marca ##21# en tu celular para desactivar de inmediato cualquier desvío de llamadas activo. ¡Comparte este ciberconsejo con tu familia y amigos, especialmente con los adultos mayores! La prevención la hacemos entre todos. 🛡️💻 #Ciberseguridad #ANCI #Ciberconsejos #EvitaEstafas #SeguridadDigital #SeguridadPública #Vishing

cont_clean: 🎣¿Sabes identificar un intento de phishing antes de que sea demasiado tarde? Toda tu información personal puede verse comprometida con un solo clic. Aprende a reconocer las señales de un ataque phishing, evitar las estafas más comunes y protegerte frente a las amenazas digitales del día a día. 🔒 Evita que un correo, un SMS o una llamada fraudulenta te sorprendan. En la #CharlaDeConcienciación "Phishing" organizado por nuestros compañeros del @ceisttalavera , vimos los tipos de phishing: correo electrónico, smishing y vishing. 📢 Nuestro #SecurityTip de la semana es: ✅Verifica el remitente real, no solo el nombre que aparece. Revisa el dominio completo y asegúrate de que corresponde a la entidad legítima. 🔗Pasa el ratón por encima de cualquier enlace antes de hacer clic y comprueba que el dominio de destino es el correcto. ⏳Desconfía de los mensajes que generan urgencia ("Tu cuenta se bloqueará en 24 horas"). Es una de las técnicas más utilizadas en phishing. 🌐Nunca introduzcas tus credenciales desde un enlace recibido de un correo. Accede siempre escribiendo la URL directamente en el navegador o utilizando tus favoritos. 📝Comprueba si hay errores ortográficos o de formatos extraños. Aunque cada vez es menos fiable, ya que el phishing generado con IA suele estar bien redactado. 📞Verifica por otro canal (llamada, Slack, Teams, etc.) si un compañero o el "CEO" solicita algo inusual por correo electrónico. Es una técnica habitual en los ataques BEC (Business Email Compromise). 🛡️Activa MFA siempre que sea posible. No evita todos los ataques de phishing, pero limita mucho el impacto si tus credenciales se ven comprometidas. 🚨Reporta cualquier correo sospechoso en lugar de eliminarlo. Así, el equipo de seguridad podrá bloquear el remitente o el dominio y proteger al resto de usuarios. #UnitelCiberseguridad #Concienciación #Phishing #Vishing #Smishing #ProtegeTusDatos

cont_clean: 3 seconds. That's all a scammer needs to sound exactly like you. I need to kill this myth: Your voice is no longer your password. Welcome to the era of the 3-second voice clone. Using AI, scammers can now scrape a tiny snippet of your audio from a LinkedIn video, a voicemail, or even a quick "wrong number" call. They then clone your voice to call your staff, sounding identical to you, and authorize "urgent" emergency payments. It's happening to Victorian SMBs right now. Here is how you protect your business: 1. THE SAFE WORD: Establish a secret verbal phrase known only to you and your authorized team for high-stakes requests. 2. MULTI-CHANNEL VERIFICATION: If "you" call asking for a transfer, your team must hang up and message you on a separate app to confirm. 3. THE 3-SECOND RULE: Treat every unexpected urgent request with extreme skepticism. At Omnicron, we harden the human element of your security. cont_clean: 📱 ¿Te han vaciado la cuenta por un SMS o una llamada? Es más frecuente de lo que parece. Y lo que muchas víctimas no saben es que el banco también puede ser responsable. El phishing bancario funciona así: recibes un mensaje, un email o una llamada que parece provenir de tu entidad. Te piden que confirmes una clave, un código o una operación. En cuestión de minutos, tu cuenta puede quedar vacía. Tú no autorizaste nada. Pero el banco, en muchos casos, deniega la devolución alegando que facilitaste tus datos voluntariamente. Lo que la ley establece es diferente. La normativa de servicios de pago obliga a las entidades financieras a devolver el importe de las operaciones no autorizadas. Solo pueden negarse si demuestran que hubo negligencia grave por tu parte, algo que los bancos aplican de forma abusiva para no asumir su responsabilidad. Si tu banco ha denegado la devolución del dinero que te robaron mediante phishing, smishing o vishing, puedes reclamar. Y reclamar tiene respaldo legal. 👉 ¿Te ha ocurrido esto? cont_clean: Il vishing bancario: come riconoscere lo spoofing del numero della banca e cosa fare nelle prime 24 ore Il telefono squilla. Sul display, il numero della vostra banca. Quello vero, salvato in rubrica. Non è la banca. È spoofing: la tecnica che falsifica numero e nome del chiamante. Non è un'intrusione nei sistemi bancari — è un trucco sul protocollo telefonico. Ma funziona, perché il numero visualizzato non è più una prova di nulla. Tre cose che una banca non fa mai per telefono: → non chiede di leggere un codice OTP appena ricevuto → non chiede di installare un'app di assistenza remota → non crea urgenza artificiale ("blocchiamo tra due minuti") Se lo sentite dire, riattaccate. Sempre. Un punto che pochi sanno: la legge (D.Lgs. 11/2010, recepimento PSD2) mette l'onere della prova sulla banca, non su di voi. Deve dimostrare lei che l'operazione era autenticata correttamente e che il codice OTP era collegato in modo univoco a quella specifica operazione (dynamic linking). Un OTP generico o riutilizzabile è già un indizio di carenza del sistema antifrode. Le prime 24 ore, in ordine: Blocco immediato di carte e home banking — telefono, poi PEC Denuncia-querela, citando esplicitamente lo spoofing Richiesta di rimborso alla banca ex art. 12 D.Lgs. 11/2010 Conservare tutto: SMS, orari, log delle chiamate Il tempo gioca due volte contro di voi: la banca ha termini stretti per rispondere, e voi perdete precisione nella ricostruzione dei fatti più aspettate. Vi è mai capitata una chiamata così, anche solo sospetta? Raccontatela nei commenti — in forma anonima, senza nomi — può aiutare qualcun altro a riconoscerla in tempo. #DirittoBancario #ABF #Vishing #TutelaConsumatori

cont_clean: Here is the structural irony of phone fraud in 2026: A disciplined automation is harder to socially engineer than a tired human. It cannot be flattered, rushed, or intimidated. It enforces the same verification rules at 2 p.m. and 2 a.m., on the first call and the four-hundredth. The rules that hold: — Verification before disclosure, every time. A perfect voice clone with wrong verifiers gets a polite dead end. — Callback-to-record. Sensitive changes confirmed by calling the number on file, not the number that called in. This one rule defeats most impersonation plays outright. — No privileged actions by voice request. Authority claims cannot unlock tools that do not exist. Average listeners can no longer reliably tell a cloned voice from a real one. Asking staff to out-listen a generative model is a contest the technology was built to make unwinnable. cont_clean: CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman nearly lost his savings in a sophisticated phone impersonation scam. He shared his experience to warn others about the scammers' professionalism. The scam began when a woman, claiming to work in his bank's "fraud protection," called him with specific information about his accounts. She claimed there was fraud at his bank and instructed him to withdraw all his funds in cash to help catch the criminals, urging him not to tell bank employees. After over an hour on the phone and a trip to the bank, Gutman grew suspicious when instructed to keep staff in the dark. He realized it was absurd for law enforcement to involve a civilian in a sting operation. Had he followed through, he could have been robbed outside the bank. Gutman advises the public to: - Hang up if someone asks for cash withdrawals in the name of investigation. - Call the official number on their debit or credit cards to verify suspicious activity. - Remember that legitimate institutions won't ask for cash withdrawals or confidentiality from bank staff. topic: IVF 等辅助生殖治疗是否应获得更多公共支持？
cont_clean: De 3 typiske fertilitetsbehandlingsformer er IVF, ICSI og IUI. IVF står for "In Vitro Fertilisering", også kendt under daglig tale som "reagensglasbefrugtning". IVF dækker over to typer: 1) den konventionelle, klassiske IVF, hvor sædceller og ægceller placeres i en petriskål. 2) ICSI, forkortet fra "Intra Cytoplasmatisk Sædcelle Injektion", eller også kendt som mikroinsemination, indebærer at én sædcelle sprøjtes med en tynd nål direkte ind i ægcellen. Således er denne type IVF altså endnu mere assisteret end den konventionelle. IUI (Intrauterine Insemination) er klassisk insemination, og foregår dermed ikke "in vitro" men derimod "in vivo". IVF, ICSI og IUI henviser til selve måden hvorpå befrugtningen sker. Ved IVF/ICSI er det (befrugtningen) en mindre del af et større billede. Først bliver kvindens æggestokke og æg stimuleret med hormoner, så der modnes flere æg end naturligt. Derefter følger en ægudtagning, hvor forhåbningen er at få flere æg ud på én gang, så man har flere forsøg til at få befrugtningen til at lykkes. Herefter følger der en protokol for forberedelsen af både æg og sædceller, som involverer rensning samt udvælgelse af celler med bedst kvalitet. Befrugtningen sker enten ved konventionel IVF eller ICSI, hvorefter udviklingen af det befrugtede æg - nu kaldet embryo - følges tæt. Når (eller hvis) embryonet har udviklet sig til blastocyst-stadiet, på ca. 5. dagen, bliver embryoet 'transfereret' ind i kvinden. Dette kaldes også ægoplægning eller embryo transfer, hvor man håber på implantation - altså at ægget sætter sig fast i livmoderen. Dette bliver typisk først bekræftet ved en efterfølgende graviditetstest. #ivftreatment #invitrofertilization #icsi #assistedreproductivetechnology #fertilitytreatment

cont_clean: 🌟 Egg Quality Is Set by Age, Not by Stimulation 🌟 Did you know? The quality of a human egg is determined by age long before any IVF treatment begins! 🥚💔 A groundbreaking study involving 12,298 embryo biopsies revealed that factors like higher doses of medication, longer stimulation, and collecting more eggs do not impact the chance of a chromosomally normal embryo. Instead, fertility medications simply rescue eggs your body would lose that month. The reassuring takeaway? Collecting a healthy number of eggs gives you more chances without lowering the quality! 🙌✨ For those on their fertility journey, remember: Your age matters more than you think! 💪 Irani M, Canon C, Robles A, et al. No effect of ovarian stimulation and oocyte yield on euploidy and live birth rates: an analysis of 12 298 trophectoderm biopsies. Human Reproduction. 2020; #FertilityFacts #IVFJourney #EggQuality #ReproductiveHealth #InVitroFertilization #InfertilityAwareness

cont_clean: This new Commentary by Fatih Aktoz, Nikolaos P Polyzos, and Christophe Blockeel explains what lies "inside the box" of regression analysis in IVF research. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rbmo.2026.105800 In IVF studies, where outcomes are influenced by many biological and clinical factors, regression models offer a structured way to address complexity. However, an adjusted result is not automatically more reliable than a crude comparison. The meaning of a regression estimate depends on the outcome chosen, the variables included, the assumptions made, and the structure of the data. Without understanding these elements, even a statistically impressive model may mislead. In the same way that a single p value cannot capture the full meaning of a study, a single regression model cannot define clinical reality. A regression model is a structured summary of data built on specific assumptions. For this reason, it should not be seen as the final answer, but as one more step toward understanding. Read the full paper for free, from our in-press articles section now https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rbmo.2026.105800 #RBMOcommentary #ClinicalAssistedReproductiveTechnology #RegressionAnalysis #Adjustment #Confounding #InVitroFertilization #OddsRatio

Then settled. The settlement required the airlines to divest some gates and slots at certain airports to enable low-cost carrier competition. The merger created the world's largest airline. In the decade following that merger and others that consolidated the industry, checked baggage fees, seat selection fees, and change fees generated tens of billions in ancillary revenue. In 2022, U.S. airlines collected approximately $7.5 billion in baggage fees alone. These fees were not standard before airline consolidation reduced competitive pressure. The Biden administration directed the DOT to crack down on what it called "junk fees" in the airline industry. The DOT finalized rules in 2024 requiring airlines to disclose fees upfront and provide refunds for canceled flights. Airlines challenged the rules. cont_clean: Booked a Phoenix or Scottsdale resort lately and the 'final' price jumped $80/night above the listing? That's now illegal. And you can get the money back. 💳 FTC's Junk Fees rule (effective May 12, 2025) + AZ Consumer Fraud Act (A.R.S. § 44-1521) require: the ADVERTISED price must include every MANDATORY fee. If you can't book without paying the fee, it has to be in the up-front price — not after your card is entered. Applies to: • Hotels + resorts (no surprise 'resort fees') • Short-term rentals (cleaning, 'destination' fees) • Live-event tickets (Ticketmaster, SeatGeek, StubHub) • Vacation rentals What's covered: resort, cleaning, service, processing, facility fees ✅ What's NOT: truly optional add-ons like parking, early check-in, upgrades. How to get a refund: 1) SCREENSHOT immediately — listed price, cart, final receipt 2) Email the vendor: 'Your advertised rate was $X. My mandatory total was $Y. Under the FTC Junk Fees rule and A.R.S. § 44-1521, please refund the difference.' Most chains refund at this step. 3) If denied: file at reportfraud.ftc.gov AND azag.gov/consumer. Cite the rule by name, attach screenshots. 4) Credit card chargeback as backstop ('service not as described'). AZ note: AG Mayes has publicly committed to enforcing this against AZ properties AND national vendors for AZ-resident bookings. You don't need to prove the company is HQ'd in AZ. Worst surprise fee you got hit with in AZ this year? "No family should have to choose between insulin and groceries," she said. ‎ ‎San Diego County saw $99 million cleared for 40,369 people. cont_clean: Your home is your sanctuary, but did you know an unpaid medical bill could actually lead to a lien on it? It's a heavy thought, but in many states, hospitals can legally place a lien on your property to secure payment for medical debt. This means if you ever try to sell or refinance your home, that debt has to be settled first. It turns a healthcare issue into a housing security issue overnight. But here's the empowering part: you have the right to fight back. Many liens are the result of bills that were never properly audited or disputed. At LTBUF 2, we specialize in catching those errors and navigating the financial aid process before things escalate. cont_clean: Medical debt is one of the leading causes of financial hardship in the United States. Connecticut has been quietly tackling the problem by purchasing and eliminating qualifying medical debt for residents. By the numbers: 🔹 Since 2024, Connecticut has erased more than $513 million in medical debt for approximately 252,000 residents. 🔹 In the latest round announced in June 2026, nearly 97,000 residents had about $315 million in medical debt eliminated. 🔹 Residents qualify if their household income is 400% or less of the federal poverty level or if their medical debt equals at least 5% of their annual income. There is no application process. Eligible debt is identified and purchased in bulk through the state's partnership with the nonprofit Undue Medical Debt. 🔹 The initiative has been funded with $6.5 million in federal American Rescue Plan Act funds approved by the state. 👀 Why We're Watching Medical debt doesn't just affect hospital bills. It can delay care, create financial stress, and make it harder for families to recover after a medical emergency. Connecticut's program has become one of the largest state-led medical debt relief efforts in the country, but not every hospital or healthcare provider participates. That means two patients with similar financial situations may have very different outcomes depending on where they received care. Question: Should medical debt be eliminated nationwide, or should responsibility remain with individual patients and healthcare providers? #connecticut #medicaldebt #healthcare #costofliving #connecticutnews

Here's what every section actually means: **Personal Information** Your name, address, employment history. Errors here are more common than you'd think — and worth disputing. **Account History** Every credit card, loan, and line of credit you've ever had. Payment history, balances, open/closed status. cont_clean: A major misconception is that the at-fault party's insurance company will pay your medical bills as they roll in. Unfortunately, that's not how it works. They only pay out in one lump sum after a final settlement is reached. So, how do you cover your treatments in the meantime? Here are the 3 most common ways: Your Health Insurance: You can use your own health insurance (private, Medicare, or Medicaid) to cover upfront costs. Keep in mind, they will likely seek reimbursement (subrogation) from your final settlement later. Medical Payments Coverage (MedPay): If you have MedPay on your own auto insurance policy, it can be used immediately to pay for medical bills up to your policy limit, regardless of who caused the accident. Medical Liens: Many medical providers understand the legal process and will agree to treat you on a "lien." This means they provide care now and agree to wait for payment out of your final case settlement. cont_clean: For years, CWFIA has challenged the narrative that SNAP fraud and improper payments were only around 1%. The latest USDA data shows why that claim never passed the smell test. In FY2025, states and territories made an estimated $10.1 billion in improper SNAP payments, with a national payment error rate of 10.62%, far above the 6% federal threshold. With total SNAP spending reaching roughly $101.7 billion, these payment errors represent about 10% of the program's entire budget. While improper payments are not the same as proven fraud, these numbers expose how misleading it has been to dismiss concerns about waste, abuse, and weak program integrity by pointing to an alleged 1% fraud rate. The reality is that billions of taxpayer dollars are being distributed inaccurately, and taxpayers deserve accountability and transparency. CWFIA has long warned that the scope of misuse in SNAP is substantially larger than official talking points suggest, and this USDA report is further evidence that the problem is far more serious than many policymakers have been willing to acknowledge. topic: 平台是否应强制移除非自愿 AI 伪造私密影像？
cont_clean: The TAKE IT DOWN Act is now in effect, marking an important step forward for survivors of image-based sexual abuse. Under this federal law, covered platforms, including certain websites, apps, social media platforms, gaming apps, and photo or video sharing sites, must provide a way to request the removal of intimate images shared without consent. This includes real images, videos, AI-generated content, and deepfakes. Once a valid request is submitted, platforms are required to remove the content and known identical copies within 48 hours. If a platform does not follow the law, a report can be made to the Federal Trade Commission at TakeItDown.ftc.gov. Image-based sexual abuse is never the survivor's fault. Survivors deserve safety, dignity, privacy, and a timely response when harm is reported. While this law is an important tool, platforms must continue doing more to prevent this abuse, stop the spread of nonconsensual content, and shift accountability away from survivors and toward those who cause and enable harm. *It is also important to know that consent to create or privately share an intimate image is not the same as consent to publish, post, or distribute it. That distinction matters. Survivors and creators deserve to have control over their own images, privacy, and safety. The law also places responsibility on covered platforms to have clear processes in place when reports are made. Platforms that allow user uploaded content should take this seriously, review their responsibilities, and ensure they are prepared to respond quickly and appropriately. For anyone whose intimate image or video has been shared without consent, this law creates a federal process that may make removal faster and more accessible. As platforms begin implementing these requirements, it can be helpful to document reports, save confirmation emails or screenshots, and keep records of where content appeared. No survivor should have to carry the burden of repeatedly proving harm. Accountability belongs with those who share, exploit, or enable the spread of nonconsensual intimate content. #ImageBasedAbuse #TakeItDownAct #SupportSurvivors #EVERYsurvivor #ACESDV

cont_clean: Current and proposed laws surrounding non-consensual content, AI-generated exploitation, are still largely reactive — meaning they allow the abuse to be uploaded first, and only after the damage is done are victims expected to chase takedowns, lawsuits, and endless copies across the internet. Laws like the Take It Down Act focus primarily on removals after harmful content has already spread online. Other proposed approaches, such as the Defiance Act, rely heavily on survivors identifying perpetrators and pursuing legal action after the abuse has already occurred. But why are we still allowing exploitative and abusive content to be uploaded in the first place? The PROTECT Act was created to focus on prevention before harm occurs through age and consent verification for uploaders to adult platforms. Because survivors should not carry the burden of cleaning up abuse that should never have been uploadable to begin with. This issue is only growing with AI and deepfake technology, and anyone can become the next victim within seconds. We need prevention. We need accountability. We need stronger digital protections before the harm happens — not after. Support the movement: @foundationra @uldouz #protectact #takeitdownact #defianceact

cont_clean: IN HER PETITION, Bollywood actress Preity Zinta claimed that several online platforms produced, uploaded, and shared AI-generated content that violated her personality rights, copyright, and resulted in immense loss of her goodwill and reputation. She further claimed infringement of her moral rights under the Copyright Act, 1957. On June 16, 2026, Tuesday, the Bombay High Court permitted Zinta, the actress turned entrepreneur, to sue Google LLC as well as various other platforms over alleged breach of her personality rights through memes, deep fake videos, manipulated images, and AI-generated chatbot personas. According to a Bar and Bench report, the court has allowed Zinta to institute a substantive civil suit against Google LLC, social media platforms, various websites, and other entities that are involved in creating, circulating, and profiting from content featuring her likeness without authorization. Bombay High Court's Take - Justice Abhay Ahuja allowed Preity Zinta's petition seeking leave of letters patent, thus permitting the actor to file an injunction suit. Since the AI-generated content and images were posted on online platforms and various respondent parties had their offices outside Mumbai, part of the cause of action had arisen outside the jurisdiction of the court, the bench noticed. By granting the leave of letters patent, the Bombay High Court stated that it would possess the authority to consider, examine, and resolve the case. After considering the submissions, Justice Abhay Ahuja accepted the arguments presented on behalf of Preity Zinta and granted her leave to file the proposed suit. In her statement to the court, Preity Zinta asserted that the improper use of her image or likeness represents a breach of her personal rights. The Bollywood actress further said that the actions in question constitute copyright infringement, damage to her reputation and goodwill, and breach of her moral rights under Section 62 of the Copyright Act, 1957. 🔗Link in bio to read the full story [ Preity Zinta petition on AI Deepfake ] #preityzinta #aideepfake

cont_clean: CHE COS'È IL REVENGE PORN? Il revenge porn (che deriva dall'inglese "vendetta pornografica") significa condividere pubblicamente materiale esplicito senza il consenso della persona coinvolta. Succede spesso quando una persona viene mollato o mollata dal proprio o propria partner e, quindi, non accettando la fine di una relazione, decide di mettere foto intime per umiliare la persona: un gesto molto brutale che può avere molte conseguenze e ripercussioni sulla vittima. Questo termine viene rimandato a vendetta, cioè, che per esempio la vittima fa qualcosa di sbagliato e che deve essere vendicata; mentre per la pornografia si parla di contenuti privati di una persona o di un attore di un'attrice. Per quanto riguarda la politica, la legge sul revenge porn viene introdotta nel 2019 e il Codice penale parla di diffusione di immagini o video sessualmente espliciti e la pena può arrivare da un'anno a sei anni di reclusione o con una multa che va dai 5 mila ai 15 mila euro, quindi non va preso alla leggera #noalrevengeporn #revengeporn #stopomobilesbotransfobia #🏳️‍🌈❤️ #🏳️‍⚧️❤️

topic: 平台是否应强制移除非自愿 AI 伪造私密影像？
cont_clean: 【AI 詐騙與 Deepfake 年代：iOS 27 防騙升級，iPhone 可能會在你轉帳前叫你諗多一下】 AI 年代嘅騙案，已經唔再只係假短訊、假網站咁簡單。 騙徒可以用 AI deepfake 扮親友、扮老闆、扮客服，甚至即時用電話一步步教你開 App、輸入資料、改密碼、做轉帳。 最難防嘅位係，整個過程入面，真正按掣嘅人係你本人。 所以傳統 Face ID、Touch ID、雙重認證未必幫到。因為系統只知道「係你本人操作」，但未必知道你係咪正被人恐嚇、催促或者教路。 Apple 喺 iOS 27 引入嘅新框架 Trust Insights，正正係想處理呢個灰色位。 不過要講清楚： 呢個功能唔係「iPhone 即時掃描詐騙 App」，亦唔係話 Apple 會判斷邊個 App 係騙案。 較準確講法係：當某個 App 整合咗 Trust Insights，而用戶正進行高風險操作時，App 可以取得一個風險訊號，判斷用戶是否可能正被詐騙者即時教路。 簡單講，Trust Insights 唔係另外喺 iPhone 入面安裝一個 AI 防騙 App，而係 Apple 將 AI 風險判斷能力放入 iOS 系統層。已整合呢個框架嘅 App，可以喺轉帳、改帳戶資料、簽文件等高風險操作前，向系統取得一個風險提示，判斷用戶是否可能正被人即時教路。 Apple 官方列出五類常見高風險操作，包括付款、更新帳戶資料、使用高成本資源、發訊息、提交表格或簽文件等。 例如你正在銀行 App 做大額轉帳，又同時似乎被人一步步指示操作，Trust Insights 可能會回傳 medium 或 high risk。App 之後可以選擇加入警告、延遲交易、要求額外驗證，甚至交由人手覆核。 私隱方面，Apple 強調 Trust Insights 不會讀取 Photos、Messages 或 Mail 內容。它主要分析互動模式、時間、操作情境同基本感應器訊號，而且裝置來源數據會在本機處理及即時丟棄。 但同時都要留意，呢個框架唔係完全離線。Apple 官方提到它會結合裝置及雲端基礎設施，評估亦可能需要網絡連線，最後結果可以結合 Apple Account 相關訊號。 今次真正值得留意嘅地方，不只是 iPhone 又多一個安全功能。 而係手機安全開始由「防止黑客入侵」，進一步走向「防止用戶被心理操控」。 AI 詐騙時代，最危險嘅未必係有人破解你部手機，而係你在壓力、恐懼或信任之下，親手完成一個錯誤操作。 如果將來銀行 App 喺你轉帳前突然彈出： 「你可能正被人即時教路，建議先諗多一下。」 你會覺得安心，定會覺得太多監控？ 👇 留言講下你點睇。 🚀 Follow 埋我哋，一齊把 AI 變成你嘅 marketing advantage。 內容由 Ai Marketer HK 提供 #AIMarketerHK #iOS27 #Apple #iPhone #TrustInsights #AIDeepfake #AI防騙 #網絡安全 #科技生活

cont_clean: Today Congress votes on H.R. 7757, also known as the Kids Act. Before they do, we need to be clear about what is at stake. This bill has gone through extensive negotiations with the same tech companies whose products have fueled the youth mental health crisis. In its current form, it risks giving those companies even more legal protection, not less. We have real solutions already on the table. The App Store Accountability Act puts age verification and parental consent where it belongs, at the app store level. The Kids Online Safety Act creates a real duty of care for platforms serving kids. These are the frameworks Congress should be building on. Passing a bill simply because it is available is not the same as passing a bill that works. Poorly designed legislation can delay real reform and leave our kids no safer than before. We are urging every Member of Congress to vote no on H.R. 7757 and commit to legislation that puts children before corporate interests. Put children first in the digital age. cont_clean: The FDA has approved a new over-the-counter naloxone nasal spray for opioid overdose. On 2 July 2026, the FDA cleared Rextovy, a 4 mg naloxone hydrochloride nasal spray. It can now be bought without a prescription. Same active ingredient as the naloxone sprays already on shelves. Same dose. No new clinical benefit. That is the point. Regulators usually approve one product for a use, and the market takes over from there. Naloxone is different. Every new approval means one more company competing for the same shelf. More competition brings prices down. Lower prices put the spray in more hands. In an overdose, minutes decide whether someone lives, so access matters more than novelty. The FDA said as much in its announcement. More formulations mean wider availability, more competition, and lower cost. For pharma teams, there is a lesson here. Life-saving generics are being treated as public health tools, not just commercial products. The FDA is using approvals to shape the market, not only to police it. Companies working on other over-the-counter transitions, such as diagnostic tests, glucose monitors, and mental health apps, should pay attention. This is a new model, and it may not stop with naloxone. The story is not that the product exists. The story is that the FDA wants many like it to exist. cont_clean: Fentanyl IV Push — Pushing too fast can stop breathing. Speed is the danger. Small doses act fast — and rapid IV push can cause respiratory depression, apnea, chest wall rigidity, and severe sedation. Every time. Watch for the early signs: slowing respiratory rate, difficult ventilation, sudden rigidity, decreased responsiveness, EtCO2 changes. Do not rely on SpO2 alone. Sedation happens before respiratory arrest. Watch the patient — not just the monitor. If toxicity occurs: stop administration immediately. Call for help. Support the airway. Bag-mask ventilate if needed. Apply oxygen. Administer naloxone per protocol. Time matters. Safety non-negotiables every time: give slowly per institutional policy. Verify dose in mcg — never mg. Airway equipment at the bedside. Monitor respirations continuously. High risk when combined with other sedatives. Save this carousel. Share it with your unit. The push rate you control is the complication you prevent. 🤍 Built for nurses. At the bedside. #mednurse #mednurseapp #fentanyl #ivpush #highalertmedication #opioidsafety #respiratorydepression #chestrigidity #naloxone #airwaysafety #nursingpharmacology #medicationsafety #nursesafety #bedsideskills #highalertopioid #ivpushsafety #apnea #sedationmonitoring #opioidnursing #nurseeducation #criticalcare #icunursing #errnursing #rn #registerednurse #medsurg #nursepractice #nursinglife #nursecommunity #nursingcommunity

This blog breaks down what the latest data means for real-world SUD treatment work: Fatal overdoses dropped in 2024 vs 2023 in preliminary reporting, tied to real investments in harm reduction, naloxone access, and outreach. Fentanyl is still driving the crisis and the supply is still unpredictable. Xylazine is showing up more and it complicates overdose response because it's a sedative and naloxone alone won't reverse everything. The "average" doesn't matter when your county or neighborhood is a hot spot. Use NY dashboards to track patterns and respond fast. If you're a CASAC, here's the move: Ask about the supply. Normalize test strips. Practice naloxone use. Know your local hot spots. Break the stigma around drug checking. Read it. Share it with your team. Then put the info to work in the next session. cont_clean: Fentanyl IV Push — Chest wall rigidity is the complication most nurses don't see coming. Respiratory depression gets the attention. Chest wall rigidity gets nurses off guard. Rapid IV push fentanyl can lock the chest wall — ventilation becomes difficult or impossible, the patient can't be bagged effectively. It happens fast. It can be mistaken for something else if you don't know to look for it. Watch for: slowing RR, sudden rigidity, difficult ventilation, decreased responsiveness, EtCO2 changes. Do not rely on SpO2 alone — it lags. Sedation happens before respiratory arrest. Your eyes catch it before the monitor does. If it happens: stop immediately. Call for help. Support the airway. Bag-mask if needed. Oxygen. Naloxone per protocol. Every push: slow per policy. Dose in mcg — never mg. Airway equipment ready. High risk with other sedatives. Save this carousel. The rigidity slide alone is worth sharing with your unit. The nurse who knows the less-talked-about complication is the one who catches it in time. 🤍 Built for nurses. At the bedside. #mednurse #mednurseapp #fentanyl #ivpush #chestrigidity #fentanylrigidity #highalertmedication #opioidsafety #respiratorydepression #airwaysafety #naloxone #nursesafety #nursingpharmacology #medicationsafety #ivpushsafety #apnea #sedationmonitoring #highalertopioid #opioidnursing #etco2 #nurseeducation #bedsideskills #criticalcare #icunursing #errnursing #rn #registerednurse #nursepractice #nursinglife #nursecommunity

For counselors, peers, families, and communities, this is simple: You cannot counsel someone who is dead. You cannot support recovery after a fatal overdose. Yet research has shown that PFAS ("forever chemicals") have been detected in breast milk around the world, reflecting how widespread these persistent chemicals have become. PFAS accumulate in the human body over time and can be transferred from mother to child during breastfeeding. Scientists are studying how early-life exposure may affect a baby's developing immune system, growth, and long-term health. While the evidence continues to evolve, reducing PFAS exposure has become an important public health goal. This is not a reason to avoid breastfeeding. Health authorities continue to recommend breastfeeding because its well-established benefits far outweigh the known risks. Instead, the findings highlight a much bigger issue: PFAS pollution is so pervasive that it has reached one of the most fundamental sources of infant nutrition. The real solution isn't for mothers to change, it is for governments, industries, and communities to reduce PFAS contamination at its source, protect drinking water, and phase out unnecessary uses of these "forever chemicals." Every child deserves a healthier start to life. The Impact on Medicare Enrollees About 8.8 million Americans in Medicare are prescribed at least one of the 10 discounted drugs, according to government estimates. Protecting Social Security Their campaign platforms both emphasize that they would protect Social Security, the largest source of retirement income for most older people. But there are several key differences in what they would change about the program. Harris: Protect Social Security and Increase Payroll Taxes for High-Earners Consistent with the platform she and current President Joe Biden ran on in 2020, Harris is focused on defending the Social Security program against "attacks" from the Republican Party — specifically, any changes that would reduce benefits or increase the retirement age. The platform notes that Trump once called for increasing the retirement age from 67 to 70 (but it's a position he no longer supports). To ensure Social Security can continue paying scheduled benefits long into the future, Harris calls for "making millionaires and billionaires pay their fair share in taxes." Social Security's trust funds are on track to run out of money in about a decade, and in order to avoid benefit cuts, lawmakers would need to enact policy changes to increase revenues. Raising taxes is one of those potential solutions. cont_clean: President Donald Trump signed an executive order May 12 that he said would lower prescription drug costs — an issue the U.S. bishops have flagged in recent years — but the exact potential impact of the order was the subject of debate. Trump signed an executive order to implement what is known as the "most favored nation" policy, which he said would tie the prices of some drugs in the U.S. to lower prices abroad, such as in Europe. "Basically, what we're doing is equalizing," Trump said at an event in the Roosevelt Room of the White House. "We are going to pay the lowest price there is in the world. We will get whoever is paying the lowest price, that's the price that we're going to get." The order sets a 30-day deadline for drug companies to voluntarily lower their prices, asking that the U.S. pay the same amount that other wealthy countries pay for similar medications, and offers the White House's support for these pharmaceutical companies to seek higher prices abroad. Officials said they would pursue other regulatory options if the companies did not comply. Stephen J. Ubl, PhRMA's chief executive, argued in a statement, "To lower costs for Americans, we need to address the real reasons U.S. prices are higher: foreign countries not paying their fair share and middlemen driving up prices for U.S. patients." "The Administration is right to use trade negotiations to force foreign governments to pay their fair share for medicines. U.S. patients should not foot the bill for global innovation," Ubl said. cont_clean: President Trump has signed an executive order to lower prescription drug prices by tying US medicine costs to lower international prices, aiming to reduce costs by up to 59%. This revives and expands the "most favored nation" policy from his previous term, addressing the fact that US drug prices are nearly three times higher than in 33 other high-income countries according to a 2024 RAND Corporation report. The high costs in the US stem from a complex reimbursement system where pharmaceutical companies set prices negotiated by private insurers, rather than government-negotiated price caps common in other nations. Private insurers typically rely on pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) with limited transparency, while drug makers defend higher US prices as necessary for funding research and development. While branded drugs are more expensive in the US, the system shows some efficiency with unbranded generic medications, which account for 90% of prescription volumes compared to 41% in other advanced nations. These generics tend to cost American patients about two-thirds of what other countries pay, creating a more nuanced pricing landscape than initially apparent. The new executive order requires drug makers to meet price targets within 30 days and aims to restrict the role of PBMs by creating mechanisms for patients to purchase drugs directly from manufacturers. cont_clean: The Insulin Cost $300. Across the Border, It Was $30. The molecule is identical. The manufacturer is often the same. The price is not. Americans pay more for insulin than patients in any other developed country. A 2021 RAND Corporation study found that U.S. insulin prices were nearly ten times higher than in 32 other high-income nations. The three dominant insulin manufacturers — Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk, and Sanofi — control approximately 90% of the global insulin market. For decades, those companies raised insulin list prices in coordinated, near-simultaneous increments — a practice that drew FTC scrutiny. The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 capped insulin costs at $35 per month for Medicare beneficiaries. It did not cap prices for patients with private insurance or no insurance. All three manufacturers later voluntarily capped their insulin prices at $35 per month broadly — under significant congressional and public pressure. Before those caps, Americans were rationing insulin. A documented and studied phenomenon: patients skipping doses, splitting pens, crossing into Canada with coolers to buy a month's supply. The caps are now in place. They came after decades of price increases. And they were voluntary — not legally required. Follow Off Limits America. We show you the system. topic: 政府是否应进一步压低处方药价格？
cont_clean: 💉 Americans pay 10x more for insulin than people in other wealthy countries. Not a different product. The same drug. In the U.S., the average manufacturer price sits at roughly $98 per unit. Across 33 other high-income nations? $8.81. Countries like Turkey and Mexico often pay less than $8. That gap forces millions of people with diabetes to choose between insulin and rent. Between insulin and food. 🌍 So why is it so expensive here? Most wealthy nations let governments negotiate prices directly and set caps. The U.S. has no equivalent system. Instead, private insurers and pharmaceutical middlemen control pricing, producing inflated list prices and rebate arrangements that are nearly impossible to follow from the outside. 📉 Things have moved slightly. Federal legislation now caps out-of-pocket insulin costs at $35/month for Medicare seniors. Several manufacturers have voluntarily lowered list prices. That helps a lot of people. But the structural problems that allowed a century-old drug to cost ten times more here than anywhere else in the developed world? Largely untouched. 💡 Facts checked by @omgfacts Sources via link in bio. Save this and send it to someone who needs to see it. #omgfacts #healthcare #diabetes #insulin #insulinprices #healthcarecosts #diabetesawareness #medicaldebt #publichealth

cont_clean: Do you market your business? 🤔 If you do, it's likely the PECR applies to you 🤓 The regulations are derived from the 'e-privacy Directive' in European law, and run alongside the GDPR ⚖️ They specifically relate to rights in relation to electronic communications, and apply even where you are NOT collecting personal data 💻 Although the EU is planning to replace the directive, it currently applies if you 👇: 🔑market by phone, email, text or fax 🔑use cookies on your website 🔑compile a telephone directory In some cases, for specific businesses such as network and service providers, only the PECR rules will apply, and not those of the GDPR 👩‍💻 Breaching the PECR can result in both criminal and non-criminal enforcement, audit, and also monetary fines of up to £500,000 against both companies and directors 💰 For more information on the PECR/ GDPR and how it impacts your business, visit the @informationcommissionersoffice website or click the link in our bio ⬆️

cont_clean: It's Day 32 / 90 of the #privacy101challenge. Is it necessary for schools to collect kids' biometrics? As schools are using technology to improve the quality of education, there are certain privacy concerns that come with it. Biometrics which include fingerprints, facial recognition and iris scans are being used in schools for taking attendance, lunch payments amongst other things. There are good reasons for using biometrics, one of which is unlike a student ID card, biometrics cannot be used by someone else. So no false attendance or taking someone else's lunch. However, weighing the pros and the cons, the cons far outweigh the pros. Before relying on using biometrics, it's important for technical measures to be put in place to prevent any data breach but schools lack this high level of security. Biometrics are the permanent identity of a person. They can never be changed. Imagine if hackers steal this identity and use it for malicious purposes, it dents the image and reputation of the child even before they grow to be adults. Under data protection laws, biometrics are sensitive personal data which means that they require sophisticated levels of security and appropriate safe guards for processing. A few months ago, Google agreed to settle victims with a sum of $8.75 million under a class action suit, after it violated the Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA) of Illinois. The company collected biometric data from students using its Google Workspace for Education platform without first getting consent from the students or their parents. Using biometrics should be the last resort only when it's STRICTLY necessary to use it. Parents should ask questions before granting consent: ❔Are there alternatives that are less risky? ❔What appropriate safeguards are in place to protect the data? ❔Where is the data stored? ❔Is it encrypted? ❔What is the retention policy? There are alternatives to using biometrics such as good ol' ID cards, Teacher's attendance registers and PINs or passwords. If it ain't broken, why fix it? #sensitivepersonaldata #dataprotectionlaws #dataprivacy #dataprotection #kidsprivacy #childonlineprivacyprotectionact

Processing sensitive personal data is not unrestricted. It requires specific lawful bases such as explicit consent, legal obligations, vital interests, substantial public interest, healthcare purposes, or research subject to adequate safeguards. The law recognizes that certain categories of data require enhanced protection because of their deeply private nature. For organisations, this means stronger compliance obligations, stricter accountability standards, and heightened legal responsibility. Sensitive personal data cannot be processed casually. cont_clean: There are companies whose entire business is collecting detailed information about you and selling it to whoever is willing to pay. Here is what that actually looks like and what you can do about it. Data brokers gather your name, current and past addresses, phone numbers, family members, estimated income, political affiliation, purchasing habits, and more. They pull it from public records, retailers, apps, and other brokers. Then they sell it in packages to marketers, background check services, private investigators, and anyone else who pays. This matters for your security because scammers buy these lists to make targeted attacks more convincing. When someone calls and already knows your address, your family members' names, and recent events in your life, that information came from somewhere. Data brokers are a common source. Most brokers are required to remove your information if you request it. The process is tedious because each site has its own opt-out form. Spokeo, Whitepages, BeenVerified, and Intelius are worth starting with. Search your name and work through the removal process on each one. Your information will reappear over time as new data comes in. Treat it as maintenance rather than a one-time task and revisit every six months. Each removal reduces how much is out there. Search your name on Spokeo or Whitepages this week and see what comes up. That is a reasonable place to start. #databrokers #digitalprivacy #onlineprivacy #cybersecurity #calmtechnologist

topic: 是否应加强警察问责并限制 qualified immunity？
cont_clean: 🚔 What Is Qualified Immunity? Many people have heard the term 'qualified immunity', but few understand what it actually means. Qualified immunity is a legal doctrine that can protect government officials, including police officers, from personal liability in certain civil rights lawsuits. In many cases, an officer may avoid being held personally responsible unless it can be shown that they violated a "clearly establishe" constitutional right. ⚖️ But qualified immunity is not absolute. Officers and government officials can still be held accountable when the facts and law support a civil rights claim. Every situation is unique, and the details matter. If you believe your constitutional rights were violated by law enforcement or another government official, it's important to understand your legal options and the protections available under the law. 📩 Contact Viktoria Morgan Law today for a confidential consultation. cont_clean: Groton hired an outside law firm to investigate its police. The real question isn't who the investigators are. It's who controls the investigation. The Town of Groton has retained Daigle Law Group to investigate an internal affairs complaint filed by First Amendment auditor SeanPaul Reyes following his July 2025 arrest outside the home of retired Connecticut State Police Sgt. Bryan Fahey. The incident drew national attention after video appeared to show Fahey, while armed, spitting in Reyes's face during the confrontation. Reyes originally faced multiple criminal charges. Most have since been dismissed, leaving a single charge of third-degree criminal trespass pending. Fahey was later charged with disorderly conduct. Both cases remain before the court. Now comes the internal investigation. An outside law firm can absolutely provide an objective review. It can also become another layer between government officials and public accountability. That's why the public shouldn't simply assume "outside counsel" means independence. Who determines the scope of the investigation? Who decides what evidence investigators receive? Can investigators interview anyone they believe is relevant, or only those the town identifies? Does Groton Police get an opportunity to dispute or edit factual findings before the report is released? Will taxpayers receive the full report, or only a heavily redacted version? Those answers matter far more than the firm's name. If taxpayers are paying for this investigation, they deserve complete transparency, including the engagement letter, investigative scope, billing records, body camera footage, dispatch audio, evidence preservation instructions, internal communications, and the final report. Accountability isn't created by outsourcing an investigation. It's created by allowing the evidence-not the agency being investigated-to determine the outcome. #GrotonCT #PoliceAccountability #FirstAmendment #PublicRecords #CourtWatch

cont_clean: 🚫 MYTH BUSTING MONDAY MYTH: If you have health insurance, healthcare is affordable. FACT: Nearly half of working-age Americans (46%) struggled to afford healthcare in 2025—even many who had insurance. A new survey of 10,000 adults found that healthcare affordability remains a serious challenge across the country. Researchers defined affordability problems as medical debt, trouble paying medical bills, or going without needed healthcare because of cost. The findings were eye-opening: • 39% of people with employer-sponsored insurance reported affordability challenges. • 54% of people with Marketplace coverage reported affordability challenges. • 57% of Medicaid recipients reported affordability challenges. • 60% of uninsured adults reported affordability challenges. • More than one-third of adults said someone in their family skipped needed healthcare because they couldn't afford it. The burden falls especially hard on people living with disabilities, chronic illnesses, and many Black and Hispanic families. Rural communities also reported higher rates of healthcare affordability struggles. Health insurance matters—but coverage alone doesn't guarantee affordable care. High deductibles, rising premiums, prescription costs, and medical debt continue to put financial pressure on working families. Healthcare should be accessible, affordable, and available when people need it—not delayed because of cost. cont_clean: The Monopoly Was Legal. Congress Made It That Way. In most American communities, you have one cable company. Not because the market chose it. Because local governments granted exclusive franchise agreements. Cable companies negotiated franchise agreements with cities and counties that gave them exclusive rights to operate in a territory. In exchange, they agreed to build out infrastructure and pay franchise fees. The result: geographic monopolies with no competitive pressure on price or service quality. The FCC attempted to encourage competition through open-access requirements. Cable companies challenged and largely defeated those requirements in court. In 2024, the FCC attempted to restore its authority over broadband providers under Title II of the Communications Act. The rule was immediately challenged. A federal court struck it down later that year — limiting the FCC's ability to enforce net neutrality or impose service quality requirements. In the markets where a single cable provider operates, prices for broadband service in the United States are among the highest in the developed world. The infrastructure was often built with public rights-of-way. The monopoly that operates on it was granted by the government. The rates are set by the company. Follow Off Limits America. We show you the system. You decide what it means. #CableMonopoly #NetNeutrality #FCC #BroadbandPrices #OffLimitsAmerica #FollowTheMoney #GovernmentAccountability #WeThePeople

The concern is that such "priority" connectivity could reopen India's net neutrality debate, especially if premium users get better performance while others remain on normal service. Hindustan Times reported that the feature raises questions for millions of prepaid users and could test how regulators view 5G network slicing under existing rules. topic: 是否应恢复或强化网络中立性规则？
cont_clean: Airtel just launched a "Priority Postpaid" service. The name tells you everything you need to know about what happens to everyone else. 🧵 What Airtel announced (May 19, 2026): ▪️Priority Postpaid uses advanced 5G network slicing technology to create a dedicated virtual path for postpaid users - giving them superior, more consistent speeds even when the network is under heavy traffic load. What Airtel's own FAQ quietly admits: ▪️"Deprioritization is a regular occurrence for prepaid users." During congestion - busy events, transport hubs, public areas - prepaid connections get squeezed so postpaid users get their guaranteed experience. Airtel's official line: "Your prepaid speed will not be impacted." The reality: Only when the network isn't congested. Which is exactly when it doesn't matter. The net neutrality problem: ▪️TRAI and DoT say 5G network slicing doesn't violate net neutrality as long as "basic internet service isn't compromised." But there's no defined minimum speed standard for prepaid 5G users. Smartprix analysis flags this places lower-income prepaid-dependent users at a structural disadvantage - not through explicit discrimination, but through differentiated service quality based on payment tier. This isn't new - it's escalating: ▪️Airtel has been deprioritising prepaid since its 4G Platinum launch in 2020. Priority Postpaid is the 5G-era expansion of the same playbook. Each time the technology upgrades, the gap between prepaid and postpaid widens further. What needs to happen: ▪️TRAI must establish a minimum guaranteed speed floor for all 5G subscribers regardless of prepaid/postpaid status. Without it, India's Digital India mission runs on two-speed internet - fast for those who can pay more, slow for everyone else. ✅ Verified via Airtel official press release / Airtel official FAQ / Smartprix analysis (May 19–21, 2026) [Airtel Priority Postpaid prepaid slow speeds, Airtel net neutrality India 2026, Airtel 5G network slicing prepaid deprioritization, TRAI net neutrality Airtel, Airtel prepaid users disadvantaged] #AirtelIndia #NetNeutrality #TRAI #PrepaidUsers #5GIndia

cont_clean: The open internet is starting to feel less human. Axios reported from Axios House in Cannes that media and advertising leaders are warning about bots, AI scraping, and AI-mediated consumption putting pressure on the economics of the open web. The simple version: bots and AI systems are changing how online attention moves. For years, the web was built around human clicks. Someone searches. Someone scrolls. Someone lands on a site. A publisher, creator, or business earns attention. AI changes that loop. If an assistant summarizes the answer, fewer people may click through. If bots scrape content, the creator of the work may not get the same traffic back. If more activity becomes machine-to-machine, the old signals get noisy. This matters beyond media companies. Every business that depends on being found online should be watching this. My read: the next advantage is not just posting more. It is becoming easier to verify, cite, understand, and follow across channels. Clear website. Clear offer. Clear source of truth. Owned audience. Useful content that can survive outside one algorithm. topic: 是否应恢复或强化网络中立性规则？
cont_clean: Ever had a UPI payment die at a crowded shop, or a call drop in traffic? India's 5G is blazing fast when the network's quiet and shaky when it's packed. Airtel's new Priority Postpaid is the first fix: "network slicing," a reserved lane on the same network that stays reliable even in a crush. Think of a stadium — 60,000 fans posting reels, a broadcaster's 4K feed, and police who can't afford a dropped signal, all sharing one tower with different guarantees. China's already a step ahead, slicing per-app on a single phone. It moved first because it has 4x the towers, a walled-off home-grown internet, and state-backed telcos — while India's cheap-data war left our operators with little to reinvest. The catch is net neutrality. Speeding up all video calls is fair; prioritising the one app that paid the telco is not. That's the line India has to walk — and if it gets it right, UPI, telemedicine, Kavach and small factories all win. cont_clean: 📚32. If you know anything about the early internet and the World Wide Web, you know who Tim Berners-Lee is. This book begins as a biography before evolving into a fascinating history of how the web came to be and how it grew into the internet we know today. Berners-Lee himself is an incredible person. He had countless opportunities to become unimaginably wealthy from his invention, but instead chose to make the web freely available to everyone—hence the title. It's not as though he lived modestly; he simply earned his living through a successful career in technology rather than monetizing the web itself. Throughout his life, he also remained a passionate advocate for an open web, net neutrality, and universal access. As someone who was lucky enough to experience the very early internet—dialing into local bulletin board systems with a painfully slow 14.4k modem in the early '90s—I found the history especially nostalgic. Reading about Mosaic, Netscape, early browsers, and the explosion of the web brought back memories I'd almost forgotten. What surprised me most was how much I learned. I knew the broad strokes of internet history, but this filled in countless gaps and gave me a much deeper appreciation for the people, ideas, and collaboration that built the modern web. If you have any interest in technology or internet history, I highly recommend it. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5) #BookReview #Bookstagram #BooksOfInstagram #BookLover #Nonfiction

Eminem and his daughter Hailie Jade spent $700,000 to pay off the outstanding lunch balances across schools in their home state of Michigan. 🎤💛🍽️ The initiative targeted schools where children faced the prospect of being denied hot meals or given restricted lunch options due to unpaid debt, a practice that has drawn widespread criticism from child welfare advocates across the United States. The pair carried out the gesture without seeking publicity, with the story only emerging through those connected to the schools involved. School lunch debt is a systemic issue affecting millions of American children, with the School Nutrition Association estimating that the majority of US school districts carry unpaid meal balances each year. Stories of individuals stepping in to clear these debts have consistently resonated with audiences who see food poverty in schools as one of the most preventable forms of childhood hardship. We post the best animal news and the stories that everyone is talking about! topic: 是否应扩大免费学校餐？
cont_clean: Free school meals have existed in some form in the UK for over a century, but eligibility has always been restricted to the most financially stretched households. Under the previous system, children in Universal Credit households only qualified if their household take home pay after tax and National Insurance was £7,400 a year or less. That threshold was widely criticised as too low, catching only the very poorest families while leaving out large numbers of households who were still genuinely struggling. From September 2026, that threshold is gone entirely for Universal Credit households. Every child whose family receives Universal Credit will now qualify for free school meals, regardless of how much the household earns on top of their benefit entitlement. The change was announced as part of the 2025 Budget and is expected to extend free school meals to around 700,000 additional children who were previously excluded purely because their household income sat above the £7,400 line. For families affected, the saving is real and immediate. Free school meals are worth around £500 per child per year based on current meal costs. For a family with two children both previously ineligible, that's up to £1,000 a year back in the household budget. The policy sits within a broader set of measures the government has described as part of its mission to reduce child poverty, alongside the scrapping of the two child benefit cap from April 2026 and the above inflation rise in Universal Credit standard allowances. Critics of the extension have questioned the cost at a time when the public finances are under significant pressure, and whether extending eligibility to working families on Universal Credit, some of whom earn significant incomes on top of their benefit entitlement, represents the most targeted use of the available funding. Supporters point out that food poverty and hunger in schools has been a documented problem affecting children's ability to learn and concentrate, and that the previous £7,400 threshold created an arbitrary cliff edge #UKPolitics #FreeSchoolMeals #ChildPoverty #PersonalFinance #KnowTheRules

cont_clean: Going out on protected leave, the message is reassuring and mostly true. Your job is safe, take the time you need, we will see you when you are back. What nobody hands you is a checklist for the week before you return, when the comfort of leave meets the reality of the org you are walking back into. Start with what the protection actually covers. Protected leave shields you from being punished for taking it; it does not freeze you out of a layoff that would have happened anyway. So before you go back, know your return date and the exact role you are returning to in writing, confirm your benefits did not lapse and your accruals pick back up, read the handbook on reinstatement, and keep your own record of your performance from before you left. Then treat the first week back like its own first day. Meet your manager early, on your terms, and make your value concrete again to whoever is in the room now. If anything about the return feels targeted rather than routine, that is a question for an employment attorney. Rest while you can, and walk back in knowing your rights as well as they do. #CorporateDecoder #FMLA #JobSecurity #ReturnToWork

cont_clean: Going out on protected leave, you are told the same reassuring thing everyone is told. Your job is safe, your leave is fully protected, focus on what you need to. And for the most part that is true. The fine print is narrower than the comfort. Protected leave shields you from being punished for taking it. It does not freeze you out of a layoff that would have happened anyway. If a reduction in force hits your role while you are out, the law generally lets it, as long as your leave was not the reason you were chosen. The protection is against retaliation, not against the business decision. The move is to keep your own record of your performance and your role before you go, and to ask, in writing, how any restructuring would treat someone on leave. cont_clean: We often imagine recovery as a destination. A long weekend. A vacation. A few days away from work. Then everything will be fine. But burnout rarely develops in a week, and it usually doesn't disappear in one either. When stress has become your normal, recovery requires more than time off. It requires new boundaries, healthier expectations, consistent rest, support from others, and the willingness to make changes that protect your wellbeing long after the vacation ends. That's why so many people return from time off feeling better for a moment, only to find themselves exhausted again a few weeks later. cont_clean: Burnout doesn't just take your energy. That's usually where it starts—but it's rarely where it ends. First, you notice you're tired more often. Then concentrating becomes harder. Tasks that used to take minutes take twice as long. The things you once enjoyed feel like obligations. You start canceling plans, withdrawing from people you care about, and telling yourself you'll reconnect when things calm down. Eventually, the most valuable thing begins to disappear: your time. Not just the hours spent working, but the moments that make life feel meaningful—the dinner you weren't fully present for, the hobby you stopped making time for, the conversations you were too exhausted to have, the memories that never got created because work consumed the space they needed. That's why burnout is about more than productivity. Productivity can recover. Lost energy can return. But the relationships, experiences, and parts of yourself that get pushed aside along the way are much harder to replace. Burnout doesn't steal everything at once. It takes a little here, a little there, until one day you realize it has been quietly taking pieces of your life all along. Pushing rest to the bottom of the priority list. Telling yourself, "I can handle it" as the pressure slowly builds. Then what felt temporary becomes your normal. The long days, the constant stress, the mental fatigue. Until one day, you're no longer just busy; you're exhausted. And eventually, you may find yourself feeling disconnected from the work, the people, and even the version of yourself you used to be. Burnout isn't a sudden event. If you're having a tummy tuck or muscle repair, many patients need about three weeks (21 days) before returning to work, depending on their job duties and how they're healing. Your surgeon can often complete your FMLA paperwork, but many do not complete short-term disability forms. Our team can help guide you through that process so you can focus on your recovery. When approved, short-term disability may replace a portion of your income—typically 60%–75%, depending on your employer's plan. In some cases, it may be higher. You may also want to use short-term disability during your recovery and preserve your FMLA for future needs. Once your FMLA entitlement is exhausted, it generally does not reset until the next applicable 12-month FMLA period under your employer's policy. Recover first. Return to work when you're ready. For many workers, earning minimum wage means working full-time while still struggling to afford basic necessities like housing, groceries, transportation, childcare, and utilities. Others argue that minimum wage is meant to be an entry-level starting point, not a long-term income. As the cost of living continues to rise across Canada, many people are asking: * Can someone realistically support themselves on minimum wage in your community? * Should minimum wage automatically increase with inflation? * What is the difference between a minimum wage and a living wage? * Should governments, employers, or both play a bigger role in ensuring workers can afford a decent standard of living? There are strong opinions on all sides, and this conversation affects workers, families, small businesses, and the broader economy. 👇 We want to hear from you. Do you believe your local minimum wage is enough to live on? Why or why not? Share your thoughts respectfully in the comments and let us know where you're from so others can understand the cost of living in different communities. Let's have a constructive conversation grounded in real experiences and common sense. #QuestionOfTheDay #MinimumWage #LivingWage #CostOfLiving #Canada

The **Living Wage for All Act**, introduced by Sen. Chris Murphy, proposes raising the federal minimum wage from **$7.25 an hour** to **$25 an hour** through a phased rollout. Here's how it would work: 📈 Large corporations would reach the $25 minimum by **2032**. 🏪 Smaller businesses would have until **2039** to comply. 💰 Once the $25 rate is reached, future increases would automatically adjust based on national wage growth to help workers keep up with the cost of living. Supporters say the current federal minimum wage hasn't increased since **2009** and no longer reflects today's costs for housing, groceries, healthcare, and transportation. Critics argue the proposal could increase operating costs for businesses and potentially lead to higher prices or fewer jobs. The proposal has only been introduced—it **has not become law**. cont_clean: EV Tax Credit ⚡ Up to $7,500 back—if you qualify. How the Credit Works: The federal EV tax credit offers up to $7,500 for qualifying new electric vehicles. The credit reduces your tax bill dollar-for-dollar. But not every EV or buyer qualifies. Vehicle Requirements: The vehicle must be assembled in North America and meet battery and mineral sourcing rules. Each requirement is worth $3,750. Failing one cuts the credit in half. Income Limits Apply: The credit phases out at higher incomes. Single filers above $150K, head of household above $225K, and joint filers above $300K may lose eligibility entirely. Price Caps Matter: New EVs must be priced under $55,000 (cars) or $80,000 (SUVs, trucks, vans) to qualify. Exceeding the cap disqualifies the vehicle—even if you qualify otherwise. Used EV Credit Available: Used EVs may qualify for up to $4,000. Lower price caps and income limits apply. This opens EV credits to more buyers. Contact Uncle Kam for EV tax credit guidance. Link in bio. 💬 Does your EV qualify for the credit? #evtaxcredit #electricvehicle #taxcredit #cleanenergy #unclekam

The album is a record born from urgency, from ICE raids and systemic racism to abuse of power and erased histories. She calls it "Protest meets Rhythm": music as vindication, memory, and collective dance floor. Recorded in Madrid and New York, the album brings together musicians Cortés has admired and collaborated with throughout her life, creating what she describes as a deeply joyful, high energy studio experience—part recording session, part collective ritual. Cortés says the album was initially inspired by several converging events in Eva's life: after a serious accident that resulted in three degree burns and loss of three fingers on her hand, Eva set out on a healing journey through Brazil. In the course of her travels, she met a historian who told her about La Malinche, an historic figure who, in the Spanish conquest of the Americas, was sold into slavery as a kid. Eva also learned how to kite ski on this trip, despite her missing fingers. "Those two are the only ones you need,'" her instructor told her. "A Tribo do Vento" (The Tribe of the Wind) was inspired by this experience. Eva was also inspired to write "Letters From Heaven" about the tragic murder of 14-year-old Apache Indian, Emily Pike, and ended up traveling to San Carlos, AZ, to spend a week on the Reservation. "The following month, her body was found," Cortés says. "This tragedy shook me to my core in a way I couldn't even articulate. Shortly after, I wrote the lyrics for 'Letters to Heaven.' cont_clean: ""It's like borders 2.0, okay, so borders which embrace innovation." "Yeah, they're smart borders […] primed to flex, depending on circumstances, using technology-" "Using what technology?" "I don't actually know." When watching Channel 4's 2023 docudrama Partygate, I was not expecting a brief discussion of the Johnson government's proposed 'smart borders' to stick in my mind. Though scripted to satirise and highlight the ignorance of Downing Street advisors between 2019 and 2021, the language employed here to describe the digitisation of border technologies is not unfamiliar. Concealed by connotations of advancement and efficiency, technology is often conceived to be a good thing in the general consciousness – and certainly within policy. It is a way to move forwards and not be left behind in this fast-paced world. At the very least, there is a shield of neutrality attached to technology's artificiality. Wrapped up in positive but vague allusions to intelligence and innovation, the actual details of what technology is being used in bordering practices is unknown and rarely questioned," writes Georgia Hawthorne. Before 9/11, communities on both sides of the border were interconnected along this remote stretch of river, able to cross informally to Mexico and back with little hassle. Farmers who pump water from the river don't know if they'll be able to access their pumps, and people who rely on the river for recreation fear tourists won't come if a wall is erected. The proposed stretch lies across a challenging terrain of mangrove forests, tidal creeks, swamps and embankments, where the BSF currently relies on Floating Border Outposts (BOPs) and marine patrols instead of physical barriers. The Indian Home Ministry plans to fence the West Bengal section of the border, with BSF Director General Praveen Kumar reviewing the project during his visit to the Sundarbans from July 11–14. According to officials, local communities have been informed that the project is intended to strengthen national security. However, the proposal has raised concerns among residents over land acquisition, while several hotels and lodges fear possible relocation. Authorities have said that compensation and rehabilitation measures will be provided where required. The project also faces environmental constraints, as nearly 71 kilometres of the proposed route runs alongside the Sundarbans Wildlife Sanctuary, a protected area where construction is restricted. Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis has promised comprehensive support to the victims of the flood disaster and emphasized the government's commitment to restoring what was lost. At the tourism fair in Thessaloniki, Mitsotakis announced the establishment of a fund for natural disasters and the introduction of mandatory full insurance against natural disasters for all companies.
